steps.title: Identify the context
steps.content: The plane is taking off and speeding up. The sentence asks what the plane does with its speed.

steps.title: Find the word that fits
steps.content:
"Gather" needs to mean build up or increase — the plane is accelerating for takeoff. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — Gain: "the plane begins to gain speed" — gain means to acquire or increase. A plane gains speed as it accelerates down the runway before lifting off. ✓
  • Choice B — Doubt: "the plane begins to doubt speed" — doubt means to be uncertain about. Speed is not something a plane can be uncertain of. ✗
  • Choice C — Welcome: "the plane begins to welcome speed" — welcome means to receive gladly. A plane cannot welcome speed; this is not a physical action. ✗
  • Choice D — Force: "the plane begins to force speed" — force means to compel by strength. While planes do accelerate, "force speed" is not a natural phrase for building velocity. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
